title: The Food (Special Courts) Act, 1956 (East Pakistan Act)
title_en: The Food (Special Courts) Act, 1956 (East Pakistan Act)
description: An Act to provide for setting up of Courts for speedy trial of offences in relation to foodstuffs. WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for setting up of Courts for speedy trial of offences in relation to foodstuffs; It is hereby enacted as follows:-

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent and commencement
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Act may be called the [* * *] Food (Special Courts) Act, 1956.
(2) It extends to the whole of [Bangladesh].
(3) It shall come into force at once.

provisions.title: 2. Definitions
provisions.content:
2. In this Act unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context,-
(a) “foodstuffs” and “notified order” shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in the [* * *] Control of Essential Commodities Act, 1956.
(b) “Special Magistrate” means Special Magistrate appointed under this Act.

provisions.title: 3. Offences under the Ordinance
provisions.content: 3. Notwithstanding anything contained in the [* * *] Control of Essential Commodities Act, 1956, or in any other law for the time being in force, contravention of any notified order in respect of foodstuffs made or deemed to have been made under the [* * *] Control of Essential Commodities Act, 1956, shall be tried and punished by Special Magistrates appointed under this Act and no other Court shall have any jurisdiction to take cognizance of any such offence.

provisions.title: 4. Appointment of Special Magistrates
provisions.content: 4. The Government may appoint as many Special Magistrates as it may deem necessary to try and punish offences under this Act.

provisions.title: 5. Cognizance of offences by Special Magistrates
6. Penalties
provisions.content: 6. If any person commits an offence punishable under this Act, he shall be punishable with an imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years or with fine or with any one or both of them and the Court of Special Magistrate trying such offence may direct that any foodstuffs in respect of which the said Court is satisfied that a notified order has been contravened shall be forfeited to the Government.

provisions.title: 7. Procedure for trials
provisions.content:
7. (1) A Special Magistrate may pass any sentence authorised by this Act.
(2) A Special Magistrate trying an offence under this Act shall have power to try such offences summarily and in trying such offences such Magistrate shall follow the procedure laid down in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 , for summary trials:
Provided that such Magistrate shall not be required to record more than a memorandum of the evidence.

provisions.title: 8. Inconsistency with other laws
provisions.content: 8. The provisions of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 , in so far as they are applicable and in so far as they are not inconsistent with the provisions of this Act, shall apply to all matters connected with, arising from or consequent upon a trial by a Special Magistrate appointed under this Act.

provisions.title: 9. [Repealed]
provisions.content: 9. [Repeal and saving.- Repealed by section 3 and the Second Schedule of the East Pakistan Repealing and Amending Ordinance, 1966 (East Pakistan Ordinance No. XIII of 1966).]

definitions.definition: Special Magistrate appointed under this Act.
